summary: Comprehensive backend (16 pytest cases) and frontend (Playwright) testing for the Salah Time Generator. All backend endpoints (auth, dashboard, masjids CRUD, waqth-charts CRUD + upload coverage, salah-configs upsert, generate-salah with rounding/iqamah, exports CSV/Excel/PDF) passed (16/16). All frontend flows (login render, dashboard stats, add masjid, masjid list, manual waqth chart entry, masjid detail config + generate, exports visibility, mobile sidebar toggle) passed.

minor.issue: Deleting a waqth chart does NOT cascade-delete or invalidate any salah_configs that reference it; subsequent /api/generate-salah will return 404 instead of a clearer error. Also salah_configs are not deleted on user account changes.
Export endpoints accept POST data without ownership/auth check. They take user-supplied 'data' and stream it back, so not a data-leak bug, but they should still require auth for consistency.

Inner waqth chart fetch does not filter by user_id, so a config could theoretically reference another user's chart_id (low risk since chart_id is UUID-like and config creation doesn't verify ownership of waqth_chart_id either).

issues: Avatar image src 'https://via.placeholder.com/150' returns ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED in some networks; consider replacing with a local SVG fallback or initials avatar.

action_items: Optional: add cascade cleanup of salah_configs when a referenced waqth_chart is deleted, or surface a friendlier error message in generate-salah when chart was deleted.
Optional: require auth on /api/export/* endpoints for consistency.
Optional: verify ownership of waqth_chart_id in POST /api/salah-configs and POST /api/masjids (waqth_chart_id field) so users cannot reference another user's chart.
Optional: replace via.placeholder.com avatar with local fallback to remove console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 564-line file with no separation of concerns. Recommend splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/masjids.py, routers/waqth_charts.py, routers/salah.py, services/salah_engine.py.
salah_configs Mongo upsert in POST /api/salah-configs always sets a fresh config_id on each upsert, which is fine but means the value is overwritten on every save (acceptable but document it).

generate_salah: row.get(prayer, row.get(prayer.capitalize(), '')) only matches lower-case and capitalised keys; uppercase keys (FAJR) from CSV uploads will not be picked up. Consider normalising column names on chart upload.
round_time and add_minutes_to_time use string parsing; consider using datetime for safer arithmetic and validation.

CORS: allow_origins is read from CORS_ORIGINS env var default '*' which combined with allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per spec (browsers will reject). Set explicit origins in production.
No rate limiting on auth/session exchange endpoint.
test_credentials: session_token=test_session_salah_123 (Bearer or cookie) for user_id=test-user-salah; seeded masjid_14b6ab69c3f2 and chart_e9e924a51a31.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Test artefacts (TEST_UI_Masjid, TEST_UI_Chart, chart_number=2 config) were created during testing and cleaned up via mongosh post-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 16 pytest cases pass and full UI flow (login → dashboard → masjid CRUD → waqth chart manual create → masjid detail → generate salah → exports → mobile sidebar) verified end-to-end. Use Bearer test_session_salah_123 for backend curl/pytest and add session_token cookie for browser tests. Clean up TEST_ prefixed records after writes.
rca of the issue: No issues found that block any user-facing flow. Minor improvements are listed under code review and action items.
